161: type SealInfo struct {
162: 	wrapping.Wrapper
163: 	Priority int
164: 	Name     string
165: 
166: 	// sealConfigType is the KMS.Type of this wrapper. It is a string rather than a SealConfigType
167: 	// to avoid a circular go package depency
168: 	SealConfigType string
169: 
170: 	// Disabled indicates, when true indicates that this wrapper should only be used for decryption.
171: 	Disabled bool
172: 
173: 	HcLock          sync.RWMutex
174: 	LastHealthCheck time.Time
175: 	LastSeenHealthy time.Time
176: 	Healthy         bool
177: }
178: 
179: func (si *SealInfo) keyId(ctx context.Context) string {
180: 	if id, err := si.Wrapper.KeyId(ctx); err == nil {
181: 		return id
182: 	}
183: 	return ""
184: }
185: 
186: // Access is the embedded implementation of autoSeal that contains logic
187: // specific to encrypting and decrypting data, or in this case keys.
188: type Access interface {
189: 	wrapping.InitFinalizer
190: 
191: 	Generation() uint64
192: 
193: 	// Encrypt encrypts the given byte slice and stores the resulting
194: 	// information in the returned blob info. Which options are used depends on
195: 	// the underlying wrapper. Supported options: WithAad.
196: 	// Returns a MultiWrapValue as long as at least one seal Access wrapper encrypted the data successfully, and
197: 	// if this is the case errors may still be returned if any wrapper failed. The error map is keyed by seal name.
198: 	Encrypt(ctx context.Context, plaintext []byte, options ...wrapping.Option) (*MultiWrapValue, map[string]error)
199: 
200: 	// Decrypt decrypts the given byte slice and stores the resulting information in the
201: 	// returned byte slice. Which options are used depends on the underlying wrapper.
202: 	// Supported options: WithAad.
203: 	// Returns the plaintext, a flag indicating whether the ciphertext is up-to-date
204: 	// (according to IsUpToDate), and an error.
205: 	Decrypt(ctx context.Context, ciphertext *MultiWrapValue, options ...wrapping.Option) ([]byte, bool, error)
206: 
207: 	// IsUpToDate returns true if a MultiWrapValue is up-to-date. An MultiWrapValue is
208: 	// considered to be up-to-date if its generation matches the Access generation, and if
209: 	// it has a slot with a key ID that match the current key ID of each of the Access
210: 	// wrappers.
211: 	IsUpToDate(ctx context.Context, value *MultiWrapValue, forceKeyIdRefresh bool) (bool, error)
212: 
213: 	// GetEnabledWrappers returns all the enabled seal Wrappers, in order of priority.
214: 	GetEnabledWrappers() []wrapping.Wrapper
215: 
216: 	SetShamirSealKey([]byte) error
217: 	GetShamirKeyBytes(ctx context.Context) ([]byte, error)
218: 
219: 	// GetAllSealInfoByPriority returns all the SealInfo for all the seal wrappers, including disabled ones.
220: 	GetAllSealInfoByPriority() []*SealInfo
221: 
222: 	// GetEnabledSealInfoByPriority returns the SealInfo for the enabled seal wrappers.
223: 	GetEnabledSealInfoByPriority() []*SealInfo
224: 
225: 	// AllSealsHealthy returns whether all enabled SealInfos are currently healthy.
226: 	AllSealsHealthy() bool
227: 
228: 	GetSealGenerationInfo() *SealGenerationInfo
229: }
230: 
231: type access struct {
232: 	sealGenerationInfo *SealGenerationInfo
233: 	wrappersByPriority []*SealInfo
234: 	keyIdSet           keyIdSet
235: 	logger             hclog.Logger
236: }
237: 
238: var _ Access = (*access)(nil)
239: 
240: func NewAccess(logger hclog.Logger, sealGenerationInfo *SealGenerationInfo, sealInfos []SealInfo) Access {
241: 	if logger == nil {
242: 		logger = hclog.NewNullLogger()
243: 	}
244: 	if sealGenerationInfo == nil {
245: 		panic("cannot create a seal.Access without a SealGenerationInfo")
246: 	}
247: 	if len(sealInfos) == 0 {
248: 		panic("cannot create a seal.Access without any seal info")
249: 	}
250: 	a := &access{
251: 		sealGenerationInfo: sealGenerationInfo,
252: 		logger:             logger,
253: 	}
254: 	a.wrappersByPriority = make([]*SealInfo, len(sealInfos))
255: 	for i, sealInfo := range sealInfos {
256: 		v := sealInfo
257: 		a.wrappersByPriority[i] = &v
258: 		v.Healthy = true
259: 		v.LastSeenHealthy = time.Now()
260: 	}
261: 
262: 	sort.Slice(a.wrappersByPriority, func(i int, j int) bool { return a.wrappersByPriority[i].Priority < a.wrappersByPriority[j].Priority })
263: 
264: 	return a
265: }
266: 
267: func NewAccessFromSealInfo(logger hclog.Logger, generation uint64, rewrapped bool, sealInfos []SealInfo) (Access, error) {
268: 	sealGenerationInfo := &SealGenerationInfo{
269: 		Generation: generation,
270: 	}
271: 	sealGenerationInfo.SetRewrapped(rewrapped)
272: 	ctx := context.Background()
273: 	for _, sealInfo := range sealInfos {
274: 		typ, err := sealInfo.Wrapper.Type(ctx)
275: 		if err != nil {
276: 			return nil, err
277: 		}
278: 		sealGenerationInfo.Seals = append(sealGenerationInfo.Seals, &configutil.KMS{
279: 			Type:     typ.String(),
280: 			Priority: sealInfo.Priority,
281: 			Name:     sealInfo.Name,
282: 		})
283: 	}
284: 	return NewAccess(logger, sealGenerationInfo, sealInfos), nil
285: }
286: 
287: func (a *access) GetAllSealInfoByPriority() []*SealInfo {
288: 	return copySealInfos(a.wrappersByPriority, false)
289: }
290: 
291: func (a *access) GetEnabledSealInfoByPriority() []*SealInfo {
292: 	return copySealInfos(a.wrappersByPriority, true)
293: }
294: 
295: func (a *access) AllSealsHealthy() bool {
296: 	for _, si := range a.wrappersByPriority {
297: 		// Ignore disabled seals
298: 		if si.Disabled {
299: 			continue
300: 		}
301: 		si.HcLock.RLock()
302: 		defer si.HcLock.RUnlock()
303: 		if !si.Healthy {
304: 			return false
305: 		}
306: 	}
307: 	return true
308: }
309: 
310: func copySealInfos(sealInfos []*SealInfo, enabledOnly bool) []*SealInfo {
311: 	ret := make([]*SealInfo, 0, len(sealInfos))
312: 	for _, si := range sealInfos {
313: 		if enabledOnly && si.Disabled {
314: 			continue
315: 		}
316: 		ret = append(ret, si)
317: 	}
318: 	return ret
319: }
320: 
321: func (a *access) GetSealGenerationInfo() *SealGenerationInfo {
322: 	return a.sealGenerationInfo
323: }
324: 
325: func (a *access) Generation() uint64 {
326: 	return a.sealGenerationInfo.Generation
327: }
328: 
329: func (a *access) GetEnabledWrappers() []wrapping.Wrapper {
330: 	var ret []wrapping.Wrapper
331: 	for _, si := range a.GetEnabledSealInfoByPriority() {
332: 		ret = append(ret, si.Wrapper)
333: 	}
334: 	return ret
335: }
336: 
337: func (a *access) Init(ctx context.Context, options ...wrapping.Option) error {
338: 	var keyIds []string
339: 	for _, sealInfo := range a.GetAllSealInfoByPriority() {
340: 		if initWrapper, ok := sealInfo.Wrapper.(wrapping.InitFinalizer); ok {
341: 			if err := initWrapper.Init(ctx, options...); err != nil {
342: 				return err
343: 			}
344: 			keyId, err := sealInfo.Wrapper.KeyId(ctx)
345: 			if err != nil {
346: 				a.logger.Warn("cannot determine key ID for seal", "seal", sealInfo.Name, "err", err)
347: 				return fmt.Errorf("cannod determine key ID for seal %s: %w", sealInfo.Name, err)
348: 			}
349: 			keyIds = append(keyIds, keyId)

23: // barrierTypeUpgradeCheck checks for backwards compat on barrier type, not
24: // applicable in the OSS side
25: var (
26: 	barrierTypeUpgradeCheck     = func(_ SealConfigType, _ *SealConfig) {}
27: 	autoSealUnavailableDuration = []string{"seal", "unreachable", "time"}

33: 
34: // autoSeal is a Seal implementation that contains logic for encrypting and
35: // decrypting stored keys via an underlying AutoSealAccess implementation, as
36: // well as logic related to recovery keys and barrier config.
37: type autoSeal struct {
38: 	seal.Access
39: 
40: 	barrierSealConfigType SealConfigType
41: 	barrierConfig         atomic.Value
42: 	recoveryConfig        atomic.Value
43: 	core                  *Core
44: 	logger                log.Logger
45: 
46: 	allSealsHealthy bool
47: 	hcLock          sync.RWMutex
48: 	healthCheckStop chan struct{}
49: }
50: 
51: // Ensure we are implementing the Seal interface
52: var _ Seal = (*autoSeal)(nil)
53: 
54: func NewAutoSeal(lowLevel seal.Access) *autoSeal {
55: 	ret := &autoSeal{
56: 		Access: lowLevel,
57: 	}
58: 	ret.barrierConfig.Store((*SealConfig)(nil))
59: 	ret.recoveryConfig.Store((*SealConfig)(nil))
60: 
61: 	// See SealConfigType for the rules about computing the type.
62: 	if len(lowLevel.GetSealGenerationInfo().Seals) > 1 {
63: 		ret.barrierSealConfigType = SealConfigTypeMultiseal
64: 	} else {
65: 		// Note that the Access constructors guarantee that there is at least one KMS config
66: 		ret.barrierSealConfigType = SealConfigType(lowLevel.GetSealGenerationInfo().Seals[0].Type)
67: 	}
68: 
69: 	return ret
70: }
71: 
72: func (d *autoSeal) Healthy() bool {
73: 	d.hcLock.RLock()
74: 	defer d.hcLock.RUnlock()
75: 	return d.allSealsHealthy
76: }
77: 
78: func (d *autoSeal) SealWrapable() bool {
79: 	return true
80: }
81: 
82: func (d *autoSeal) GetAccess() seal.Access {
83: 	return d.Access
84: }
85: 
86: func (d *autoSeal) checkCore() error {
87: 	if d.core == nil {
88: 		return fmt.Errorf("seal does not have a core set")
89: 	}
90: 	return nil
91: }
92: 
93: func (d *autoSeal) SetCore(core *Core) {
94: 	d.core = core
95: 	if d.logger == nil {
96: 		d.logger = d.core.Logger().Named("autoseal")
97: 	}
98: }
99: 
100: func (d *autoSeal) Init(ctx context.Context) error {
101: 	return d.Access.Init(ctx)
102: }
103: 
104: func (d *autoSeal) Finalize(ctx context.Context) error {
105: 	return d.Access.Finalize(ctx)
106: }
107: 
108: func (d *autoSeal) BarrierSealConfigType() SealConfigType {
109: 	return d.barrierSealConfigType
110: }
111: 
112: func (d *autoSeal) StoredKeysSupported() seal.StoredKeysSupport {
113: 	return seal.StoredKeysSupportedGeneric
114: }
115: 
116: func (d *autoSeal) RecoveryKeySupported() bool {
117: 	return true
118: }
119: 
120: // SetStoredKeys uses the autoSeal.Access.Encrypts method to wrap the keys. The stored entry
121: // does not need to be seal wrapped in this case.
122: func (d *autoSeal) SetStoredKeys(ctx context.Context, keys [][]byte) error {
123: 	return writeStoredKeys(ctx, d.core.physical, d.Access, keys)
124: }
125: 
126: // GetStoredKeys retrieves the key shares by unwrapping the encrypted key using the
127: // autoseal.
128: func (d *autoSeal) GetStoredKeys(ctx context.Context) ([][]byte, error) {
129: 	return readStoredKeys(ctx, d.core.physical, d.Access)
130: }
131: 
132: func (d *autoSeal) upgradeStoredKeys(ctx context.Context) error {
133: 	pe, err := d.core.physical.Get(ctx, StoredBarrierKeysPath)
134: 	if err != nil {
135: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch stored keys: %w", err)
136: 	}
137: 	if pe == nil {
138: 		return fmt.Errorf("no stored keys found")
139: 	}
140: 
141: 	wrappedEntryValue, err := UnmarshalSealWrappedValue(pe.Value)
142: 	if err != nil {
143: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal stored keys: %w", err)
144: 	}
145: 	uptodate, err := d.Access.IsUpToDate(ctx, wrappedEntryValue.getValue(), true)
146: 	if err != nil {
147: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to check if stored keys are up-to-date: %w", err)
148: 	}
149: 	if !uptodate {
150: 		d.logger.Info("upgrading stored keys")
151: 
152: 		keys, err := UnsealWrapStoredBarrierKeys(ctx, d.GetAccess(), pe)
153: 		if err != nil {
154: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to decrypt encrypted stored keys: %w", err)
155: 		}
156: 
157: 		if err := d.SetStoredKeys(ctx, keys); err != nil {
158: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save upgraded stored keys: %w", err)
159: 		}
160: 	}
161: 	return nil
162: }
163: 
164: // UpgradeKeys re-encrypts and saves the stored keys and the recovery key
165: // with the current key if the current KeyId is different from the KeyId
166: // the stored keys and the recovery key are encrypted with. The provided
167: // Context must be non-nil.
168: func (d *autoSeal) UpgradeKeys(ctx context.Context) error {
169: 	if err := d.upgradeRecoveryKey(ctx); err != nil { // re-encrypts the recovery key
170: 		return err
171: 	}
172: 	if err := d.upgradeStoredKeys(ctx); err != nil { // re-encrypts the root key
173: 		return err
174: 	}
175: 	return nil
176: }
177: 
178: func (d *autoSeal) BarrierConfig(ctx context.Context) (*SealConfig, error) {
179: 	if cfg := d.barrierConfig.Load().(*SealConfig); cfg != nil {
180: 		return cfg.Clone(), nil
181: 	}
182: 
183: 	if err := d.checkCore(); err != nil {
184: 		return nil, err
185: 	}
186: 
187: 	// Fetch the core configuration
188: 	conf, err := d.core.PhysicalBarrierSealConfig(ctx)
189: 	if err != nil {
190: 		d.logger.Error("failed to read seal configuration", "error", err)
191: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read seal configuration: %w", err)
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// If the seal configuration is missing, we are not initialized
195: 	if conf == nil {
196: 		d.logger.Info("seal configuration missing, not initialized")
197: 		return nil, nil
198: 	}
199: 
200: 	barrierTypeUpgradeCheck(d.BarrierSealConfigType(), conf)
201: 
202: 	if conf.Type != d.BarrierSealConfigType().String() {
203: 		d.logger.Error("barrier seal type does not match loaded type", "seal_type", conf.Type, "loaded_type", d.BarrierSealConfigType())
204: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("barrier seal type of %q does not match loaded type of %q", conf.Type, d.BarrierSealConfigType())
205: 	}
206: 
207: 	d.SetCachedBarrierConfig(conf)
208: 	return conf.Clone(), nil
209: }
210: 
211: func (d *autoSeal) ClearBarrierConfig(ctx context.Context) error {
212: 	return d.SetBarrierConfig(ctx, nil)
213: }
